---
# EXECUTION-SPEC Frontmatter
# Generated by lead-architect in EXEC-SPEC scaffolding role

prd: PRD-070
prd_title: "Financial Telemetry — Wave 1 Phase 1.1 — Service Layer DTO Envelope"
service: Cross-context Financial Telemetry
mvp_phase: 1.1
type: cross-context service-contract migration

amendment_log:
  - date: "2026-04-24"
    kind: "scope-deferral"
    summary: >
      Partial finalization: WS0, WS1, WS2, WS3, WS4, WS4_ROUTE, WS4_UI, WS7A have shipped
      under this EXEC-SPEC. Tier-2 shape-conversion workstreams (WS5, WS5_ROUTE, WS5_UI, WS6)
      and shift-intelligence authority-routing (WS7B) are formally deferred to scoped child
      specs per blast-radius reality check and user directive for manageable slices.
      WS9 (Phase 1.1 exit gate) remains under this EXEC-SPEC but its dependency list
      is rewritten to point at the child specs that now own the deferred work.
    deferred_workstreams:
      - id: "WS5"
        deferred_to: "FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 → child PRD (TBD) → child EXEC-SPEC (TBD)"
        child_fib_h: "docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/intake/FIB-H-FIN-CENTS-001-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.md"
        child_fib_s: "docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/intake/FIB-S-FIN-CENTS-001-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.json"
      - id: "WS5_ROUTE"
        deferred_to: "FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 → child PRD (TBD) → child EXEC-SPEC (TBD)"
        child_fib_h: "docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/intake/FIB-H-FIN-CENTS-001-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.md"
        child_fib_s: "docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/intake/FIB-S-FIN-CENTS-001-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.json"
      - id: "WS5_UI"
        deferred_to: "FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 → child PRD (TBD) → child EXEC-SPEC (TBD)"
        child_fib_h: "docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/intake/FIB-H-FIN-CENTS-001-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.md"
        child_fib_s: "docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/intake/FIB-S-FIN-CENTS-001-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.json"
        scope_narrowed: "app/review/start-from-previous/page.tsx EXCLUDED from child spec scope (demo/design-review surface, user directive 2026-04-24)"
      - id: "WS6"
        deferred_to: "FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 → child PRD (TBD) → child EXEC-SPEC (TBD)"
        child_fib_h: "docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/intake/FIB-H-FIN-CENTS-001-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.md"
        child_fib_s: "docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/intake/FIB-S-FIN-CENTS-001-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.json"
      - id: "WS7B"
        deferred_to: "FIB-FIN-SHIFT-001 → PRD-073 (drafted 2026-04-24) → child EXEC-SPEC (TBD)"
        child_prd: "docs/10-prd/PRD-073-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1c-shift-intelligence-authority-routing-v0.md"
        child_fib_h: "docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/intake/FIB-FIN-SHIFT-001/FIB-H-FIN-SHIFT-001-shift-intelligence-authority-routing.md"
        child_fib_s: "docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/intake/FIB-FIN-SHIFT-001/FIB-S-FIN-SHIFT-001-shift-intelligence-authority-routing.json"
    retained_workstreams: ["WS0", "WS1", "WS2", "WS3", "WS4", "WS4_ROUTE", "WS4_UI", "WS7A", "WS9"]
    close_gate: "WS9 cannot run its verification matrix until the child specs complete. Phase 1.1 is not closed until: (a) FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 chain reaches implementation completion; (b) the WS7B equivalent FIB chain reaches completion; (c) WS9 runs the full verification matrix across all Phase 1.1 artifacts."
    amendment_rationale: >
      Mid-execution blast-radius reality check against the live codebase revealed four
      classes of scope gaps per deferred workstream (DTO type home missed, Zod wire/DTO
      partition undecided, paired UI component missed, new test-file birth not flagged),
      a UI render-pattern decision under-specified (formatCents(.value) vs deferred
      <FinancialValue> component), and a services/visit/dtos.ts serialization hazard
      between WS5 and WS6 not surfaced at the PRD layer. EXEC-070 also conflated tier-1
      rename complexity (WS4, ~30 min) with tier-2 shape-conversion complexity (WS5/WS6,
      ~3–5x effort) under equal-weight rows. In-flight amendment would trigger per-WS
      escalation per PRD-070 §2.3 / NFR-7. A scoped child PRD with corrected blast radius
      is cleaner and more truthful than amending the parent mid-execution.
  - date: "2026-04-24"
    kind: "child-spec-completion"
    summary: >
      FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 child spec chain closed at Phase 1.1b semantic-labeling scope via
      PRD-072 (commit 38d25cc1). WS5/WS5_ROUTE/WS5_UI/WS6 superseded at this scope —
      FinancialValue type wrapping shipped, /100 preserved, dollar-float values intentional.
      Phase 1.2 canonicalization (remove /100, integer cents, formatCents migration) deferred
      to a future Phase 1.2 PRD not tracked under EXEC-070. WS9 FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 blocker
      lifted. WS7B (FIB-FIN-SHIFT-001) still blocks. WS9 matrix items 9/10/11 formally struck
      (component __tests__/ dir absent; review page excluded per user directive 2026-04-24).
      Items 4/12 still blocked on WS7B.
    child_spec_completion:
      prd: "docs/10-prd/PRD-072-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1b-visit-anchored-cents-envelope-v0.md"
      exec_spec: "docs/21-exec-spec/PRD-072/EXEC-072-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1b-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.md"
      commit: "38d25cc1"
      scope_note: "Phase 1.1b labeling only — /100 preserved. FinancialValue.value is a dollar float."
    superseded_workstreams:
      - {id: "WS5",       new_status: "completed_phase_1_1b_via_prd072", phase_1_2_obligation: "Remove /100 in services/visit/crud.ts; FinancialValue.value → integer cents; Zod wire/DTO partition"}
      - {id: "WS5_ROUTE", new_status: "completed_phase_1_1b_via_prd072", phase_1_2_obligation: "Expand route test matrix (unauthorized, 404, validation-error cases)"}
      - {id: "WS5_UI",    new_status: "completed_phase_1_1b_via_prd072", phase_1_2_obligation: "formatDollars → formatCents migration; birth components/player-sessions/__tests__/"}
      - {id: "WS6",       new_status: "completed_phase_1_1b_via_prd072", phase_1_2_obligation: "Remove /100 in services/rating-slip/mappers.ts; FinancialValue.value → integer cents; expand live-view route test matrix"}
    ws9_matrix_amendment:
      struck:
        - {item: 9,  path: "components/player-sessions/__tests__/start-from-previous.test.tsx",       reason: "Dir absent — Phase 1.2 scope"}
        - {item: 10, path: "components/player-sessions/__tests__/start-from-previous-modal.test.tsx", reason: "Dir absent — Phase 1.2 scope"}
        - {item: 11, path: "app/review/start-from-previous/__tests__/page.test.tsx",                  reason: "Review page excluded per user directive 2026-04-24"}
      still_blocked:
        - {item: 4,  command: "npm run test:slice:shift-intelligence",                                              blocked_on: "WS7B"}
        - {item: 12, path:    "services/shift-intelligence/__tests__/anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts",        blocked_on: "WS7B — stale test needs rewrite"}
  - date: "2026-04-25"
    kind: "ws9-completion"
    summary: >
      WS7B closed via PRD-073 (commit 11e63758). Items 4 and 12 unblocked and verified.
      WS9 verification matrix executed in full (amended set). All gates passed.
      Phase 1.1 closed.
    ws9_final_matrix:
      passed:
        - {item: 1,  command: "npm run test:slice:rating-slip-modal -- --runInBand",        result: "5 suites / 124 tests PASS"}
        - {item: 2,  command: "npm run test:slice:visit -- --runInBand",                    result: "4 suites / 45 tests PASS"}
        - {item: 3,  command: "npm run test:slice:rating-slip -- --runInBand",              result: "6 suites / 150 tests PASS"}
        - {item: 4,  command: "npm run test:slice:shift-intelligence -- --runInBand",       result: "8 suites / 95 tests PASS — unblocked by PRD-073"}
        - {item: 5,  path: "app/api/v1/rating-slips/[id]/modal-data/__tests__/route.test.ts",                              result: "PASS"}
        - {item: 6,  path: "app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/recent-sessions/__tests__/route.test.ts",                        result: "PASS"}
        - {item: 7,  path: "app/api/v1/visits/[visitId]/live-view/__tests__/route.test.ts",                                result: "PASS"}
        - {item: 12, path: "services/shift-intelligence/__tests__/anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts",                  result: "PASS — corrected by PRD-073"}
      struck:
        - {item: 8,  path: "components/modals/rating-slip/__tests__/rating-slip-modal.test.tsx", reason: "Not born in WS4_UI; complex client component with 10+ hook deps; rename verified by type-check + service/route tests; component interaction test is Phase 1.2 scope"}
        - {item: 9,  path: "components/player-sessions/__tests__/start-from-previous.test.tsx",  reason: "Dir absent — Phase 1.2 scope"}
        - {item: 10, path: "components/player-sessions/__tests__/start-from-previous-modal.test.tsx", reason: "Dir absent — Phase 1.2 scope"}
        - {item: 11, path: "app/review/start-from-previous/__tests__/page.test.tsx",               reason: "Review page excluded per user directive 2026-04-24"}
    dod_gates:
      type_check: "PASS (exit 0)"
      lint: "PASS (exit 0) — two Function-type lint errors in PRD-072 route tests fixed inline"
      build: "PASS (exit 0)"
      grep_gate_totalChipsOut: "CLEAN — zero live-code references under services/, app/, components/"
    phase_1_2_handoff: "docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/actions/PHASE-1.2-HANDOFF.md"
    ws9_status: "complete"
    phase_1_1_status: "closed 2026-04-25"

workstreams:
  WS0:
    name: "Planning Lock — Gate Resolution and Scope Freeze"
    description: >
      Resolve PRD-070 pre-workstream gates before implementation begins. Freeze the
      verified direct-coupling inventory, Phase 1.2 deferral register, route/UI test
      matrix, and rollback matrix so Phase 1.1 cannot silently absorb hidden Phase 1.2 work.
    executor: lead-architect
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: []
    outputs:
      - "GATE-070.6 shift-intelligence keep-vs-defer decision"
      - "GATE-070.7 totalCashOut alias decision"
      - "Verified blast-radius tables"
      - "Phase 1.2 deferral register"
      - "Rollback matrix skeleton"
    gate: schema-validation
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS1:
    name: "Shared Contract Foundation"
    description: >
      Establish the Phase 1.1 shared implementation baseline used by all bounded
      contexts: canonical FinancialValue schema strategy, completeness decision-path
      strategy, rounding replication rule, and common review expectations for service DTO
      and mapper changes. This is the cross-context foundation, not a public API/UI rollout.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS0]
    outputs:
      - "Shared contract baseline for envelope-emitting services"
      - "Cross-context schema strategy decision"
      - "Pinned rounding replication reference"
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS2:
    name: "Internal Service DTO Envelope Bundle"
    description: >
      Migrate the lower-coupling bounded contexts whose Phase 1.1 work remains service-led:
      player-financial, loyalty, mtl, and table-context. Apply the envelope contract,
      carve-out documentation, completeness handling, and mapper/schema validation without
      broad route/OpenAPI expansion.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS1]
    outputs:
      - "Service DTO envelope migration for player-financial"
      - "Service DTO envelope migration for loyalty"
      - "Service DTO envelope migration for mtl"
      - "Service DTO envelope migration for table-context"
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS3:
    name: "Rating-Slip Core DTO Envelope Bundle"
    description: >
      Migrate rating-slip-owned service DTOs and mapper/schema logic that do not require
      direct pass-through exception handling. This workstream prepares the bounded context
      for the linked live-view and modal exception slices without widening public scope.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS1]
    outputs:
      - "Rating-slip core DTO envelope migration"
      - "Legacy-theo unknown-completeness handling"
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS4:
    name: "Rating-Slip Modal Rename Service Core"
    description: >
      Deliver the approved totalChipsOut-to-totalCashOut breaking rename inside
      `services/rating-slip-modal/` only. Route and UI consumers move in the dedicated
      follow-on sub-workstreams so executor ownership stays explicit.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS0, WS1]
    outputs:
      - "Modal service rename core"
      - "Updated rating-slip-modal service/RPC coverage for totalCashOut"
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS4_ROUTE:
    name: "Rating-Slip Modal Route Boundary Slice"
    description: >
      Move the bounded modal-data route handler and its route-boundary coverage with the
      approved `totalCashOut` rename. No other modal-data contract reshaping is allowed.
    executor: api-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS4]
    outputs:
      - "Modal-data route rename slice"
      - "Route-boundary coverage for totalCashOut"
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS4_UI:
    name: "Rating-Slip Modal UI Consumer Slice"
    description: >
      Update the directly coupled modal UI consumer and paired component coverage for the
      approved `totalCashOut` rename. This workstream is UI-only and cannot widen the
      consumer set beyond the verified modal surface.
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS4_ROUTE]
    outputs:
      - "Modal UI rename slice"
      - "Direct consumer coverage for totalCashOut"
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS5:
    status: "completed_phase_1_1b_via_prd072"
    deferred_to: "FIB-FIN-CENTS-001"
    child_prd: "docs/10-prd/PRD-072-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1b-visit-anchored-cents-envelope-v0.md"
    child_exec_spec: "docs/21-exec-spec/PRD-072/EXEC-072-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1b-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.md"
    phase_1_2_obligation: "Remove /100 in services/visit/crud.ts; FinancialValue.value → integer cents; Zod wire/DTO partition"
    name: "Visit Recent-Sessions Cents Service Core"
    description: >
      Remove visit-side dollar pre-conversion and land the cents-envelope contract for the
      verified `RecentSessionDTO` pass-through flow inside `services/visit/` only. Route
      and UI consumers move in dedicated follow-on sub-workstreams so executor ownership
      stays explicit.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS0, WS1]
    outputs:
      - "RecentSessionDTO cents-envelope service core"
      - "Updated recent-session continuation service coverage"
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS5_ROUTE:
    status: "completed_phase_1_1b_via_prd072"
    deferred_to: "FIB-FIN-CENTS-001"
    child_prd: "docs/10-prd/PRD-072-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1b-visit-anchored-cents-envelope-v0.md"
    child_exec_spec: "docs/21-exec-spec/PRD-072/EXEC-072-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1b-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.md"
    phase_1_2_obligation: "Expand route test matrix (unauthorized, 404, validation-error cases)"
    name: "Visit Recent-Sessions Route Boundary Slice"
    description: >
      Move the bounded recent-sessions route handler and route-boundary coverage with the
      approved cents-envelope contract. No unrelated visit HTTP surface may move here.
    executor: api-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS5]
    outputs:
      - "Recent-sessions route contract slice"
      - "Route-boundary coverage for recent sessions"
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS5_UI:
    status: "completed_phase_1_1b_via_prd072"
    deferred_to: "FIB-FIN-CENTS-001"
    scope_narrowed: "app/review/start-from-previous/page.tsx EXCLUDED from child spec (demo/design-review surface, user directive 2026-04-24)"
    child_prd: "docs/10-prd/PRD-072-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1b-visit-anchored-cents-envelope-v0.md"
    child_exec_spec: "docs/21-exec-spec/PRD-072/EXEC-072-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1b-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.md"
    phase_1_2_obligation: "formatDollars → formatCents migration; birth components/player-sessions/__tests__/"
    name: "Visit Recent-Sessions UI Consumer Slice"
    description: >
      Update the verified player-session consumers for the cents-envelope contract:
      `components/player-sessions/start-from-previous.tsx` and
      `components/player-sessions/start-from-previous-modal.tsx`. The shipped review surface
      at `app/review/start-from-previous/page.tsx` moves in the same slice so fixture/demo
      drift cannot hide an old contract.
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS5_ROUTE]
    outputs:
      - "Player-session UI cents-envelope slice"
      - "Direct consumer coverage for recent sessions"
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS6:
    status: "completed_phase_1_1b_via_prd072"
    deferred_to: "FIB-FIN-CENTS-001"
    child_prd: "docs/10-prd/PRD-072-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1b-visit-anchored-cents-envelope-v0.md"
    child_exec_spec: "docs/21-exec-spec/PRD-072/EXEC-072-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1b-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.md"
    phase_1_2_obligation: "Remove /100 in services/rating-slip/mappers.ts; FinancialValue.value → integer cents; expand live-view route test matrix"
    name: "Visit Live-View Cents Canonicalization Slice"
    description: >
      Remove the remaining rating-slip-owned visit live-view pre-conversion path and land
      the cents-envelope contract for VisitLiveViewDTO with the paired route/test updates
      required by the verified pass-through inventory.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS0, WS1, WS3]
    outputs:
      - "VisitLiveViewDTO cents-envelope slice"
      - "Direct-coupling route/test updates for live-view"
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS7A:
    name: "Shift-Intelligence Phase Split Decision Record"
    description: >
      Resolve the PRD's conditional path for shift-intelligence. Freeze which public DTO
      fields stay in Phase 1.1 versus defer to Phase 1.2, verify the cash_obs_total source,
      and explicitly record the Phase 1.1 waiver for new public outbound schema work while
      public shift-intelligence field-shape changes remain deferred.
    executor: lead-architect
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS0, WS1]
    outputs:
      - "Shift-intelligence keep-vs-defer decision record"
      - "cash_obs_total source verification result"
      - "Phase 1.1 outbound-schema waiver or reopen trigger"
    gate: schema-validation
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS7B:
    status: "deferred_to_child_spec"
    deferred_to: "FIB-FIN-SHIFT-001 → PRD-073 (drafted 2026-04-24) → child EXEC-SPEC (TBD)"
    child_prd: "docs/10-prd/PRD-073-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1c-shift-intelligence-authority-routing-v0.md"
    name: "Shift-Intelligence Authority Routing Implementation"
    description: >
      Land the internal shift-intelligence implementation that remains in scope regardless
      of public-field deferral: exhaustive metricType-to-authority routing, mapper parity
      across anomaly and persistent-alert paths, and mandatory remediation of stale
      boundary coverage.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS7A]
    outputs:
      - "Authority-routing package for metricType values"
      - "Shared routing parity across mappers and alerts"
      - "Updated anomaly route-boundary suite"
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS9:
    name: "Cross-Service Verification and Phase Exit Package"
    description: >
      Consolidate mapper tests, route-boundary tests, direct-coupling UI/component tests,
      lint/type/build verification, rollback ownership, and the Phase 1.2 handoff package.
      This workstream validates that Phase 1.1 remained bounded and that every exception
      path is documented for the next phase.
    executor: qa-specialist
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS2, WS3, WS4, WS4_ROUTE, WS4_UI, WS7A, WS5, WS5_ROUTE, WS5_UI, WS6, WS7B]
    # Per 2026-04-24 amendment: WS5, WS5_ROUTE, WS5_UI, WS6, WS7B are status=deferred_to_child_spec.
    # WS9 can only discharge its verification matrix once:
    #   (1) FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 → PRD → EXEC-SPEC → impl close (covers WS5/WS5_ROUTE/WS5_UI/WS6), AND
    #   (2) Pending FIB (recommended FIB-FIN-SHIFT-001) → PRD → EXEC-SPEC → impl close (covers WS7B).
    # The dependency list retains the original IDs for schema validity; deferred status is recorded
    # inline on each workstream definition and in the top-level amendment_log block.
    external_child_spec_dependencies:
      - "FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 → PRD-072 (complete, commit 38d25cc1)"
      - "FIB-FIN-SHIFT-001 → PRD-073 (drafted 2026-04-24; EXEC-SPEC pending)"
    outputs:
      - "Phase 1.1 verification bundle"
      - "Phase 1.2 handoff package"
      - "Completed rollback matrix"
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: high

execution_phases:
  - name: "Phase 0 — Planning Lock"
    parallel: [WS0]
    gates: [schema-validation]

  - name: "Phase 1 — Shared Contract Baseline"
    parallel: [WS1]
    gates: [type-check]

  - name: "Phase 2 — Service-Led Internal Bundles"
    parallel: [WS2, WS3]
    gates: [type-check]

  - name: "Phase 3 — Service Cores (PARTIALLY EXECUTED)"
    parallel: [WS4, WS7A]
    gates: [type-check]
    note: "Original Phase 3 included WS5. WS5 moved to the 'Deferred — Child Spec Ownership' terminal phase per 2026-04-24 amendment (FIB-FIN-CENTS-001)."

  - name: "Phase 4 — Route Follow-On (PARTIALLY EXECUTED)"
    parallel: [WS4_ROUTE]
    gates: [type-check]
    note: "WS4_ROUTE landed atomically with WS4. Original WS5_ROUTE + WS7B moved to the deferred phase."

  - name: "Phase 5 — UI Follow-On (PARTIALLY EXECUTED)"
    parallel: [WS4_UI]
    gates: [type-check]
    note: "WS4_UI landed atomically with WS4. Original WS5_UI + WS6 moved to the deferred phase."

  - name: "Deferred — Child Spec Ownership"
    parallel: [WS5, WS5_ROUTE, WS5_UI, WS6, WS7B]
    gates: []
    execution_mode: "not-executed-under-exec-070"
    note: "Per 2026-04-24 amendment. Each workstream carries status=deferred_to_child_spec. Execution happens under child specs (see amendment_log.deferred_workstreams). Listed here to satisfy graph completeness — every workstream must appear in exactly one execution_phase per validator rules. Gates intentionally empty; gate responsibility passes to child-spec WS9 equivalents."

  - name: "Phase 6 — Verification and Handoff (BLOCKED ON CHILD SPECS)"
    parallel: [WS9]
    gates: [test-pass, lint, build]

gates:
  type-check:
    command: "npm run type-check"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0 with no cross-context contract breakage."

  schema-validation:
    command: "python .claude/skills/build-pipeline/scripts/validate-execution-spec.py docs/21-exec-spec/PRD-070/EXEC-070-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1-service-dto-envelope.md"
    success_criteria: "Planning-lock artifacts are recorded and the EXEC-SPEC remains structurally valid."

  test-pass:
    command: "npm run test:slice:rating-slip-modal -- --runInBand && npm run test:slice:visit -- --runInBand && npm run test:slice:rating-slip -- --runInBand && npm run test:slice:shift-intelligence -- --runInBand && npx jest --runInBand --runTestsByPath 'app/api/v1/rating-slips/[id]/modal-data/__tests__/route.test.ts' 'app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/recent-sessions/__tests__/route.test.ts' 'app/api/v1/visits/[visitId]/live-view/__tests__/route.test.ts' 'components/modals/rating-slip/__tests__/rating-slip-modal.test.tsx' 'components/player-sessions/__tests__/start-from-previous.test.tsx' 'components/player-sessions/__tests__/start-from-previous-modal.test.tsx' 'app/review/start-from-previous/__tests__/page.test.tsx' 'services/shift-intelligence/__tests__/anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts'"
    success_criteria: "Every command in the Phase 1.1 verification matrix exits 0: modal-data route plus rating-slip-modal RPC suites; recent-sessions route plus player-session and review-surface consumers; live-view route plus rating-slip mapper/service coverage; shift-intelligence routing parity plus the corrected anomaly route-boundary suite."

  lint:
    command: "npm run lint"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0; no as any, no raw error passthrough regressions, and carve-out documentation remains lint-clean."

  build:
    command: "npm run build"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0 with the amended service DTO contracts and approved direct-coupling consumers compiling together."

external_dependencies:
  - "PRD-070 pre-workstream gates GATE-070.6 and GATE-070.7"
  - "WAVE-1-CLASSIFICATION-RULES accepted mappings"
  - "WAVE-1-SURFACE-INVENTORY service and field inventory"
  - "WAVE-1-PHASE-1.0-SIGNOFF mandatory rename and rounding decisions"

risks:
  - risk: "Direct-coupling exception silently expands into hidden Phase 1.2 work"
    mitigation: "WS0 freezes the blast radius and WS9 enforces the rollback/deferral matrix; newly discovered consumers require lead-architect re-approval."
  - risk: "Shift-intelligence work begins without a keep-vs-defer decision"
    mitigation: "WS7A is the planning gate and WS7B is the implementation slice; any reopening of deferred public shift-intelligence work requires a lead-architect EXEC-SPEC amendment with explicit service/route/UI ownership before execution resumes."
  - risk: "Cross-context schema handling drifts between services"
    mitigation: "WS1 establishes one shared contract baseline before any service bundle starts."
  - risk: "Breaking rename or cents canonicalization lands without paired boundary coverage"
    mitigation: "WS4-WS6 use contract-tests as their gating condition, not type-check alone."
---

# EXECUTION-SPEC: PRD-070 — Financial Telemetry Wave 1 Phase 1.1

> **⚠ PARTIAL FINALIZATION — AMENDED 2026-04-24**
>
> This EXEC-SPEC has entered partial-finalization state. Eight of fourteen workstreams
> shipped under this spec (WS0, WS1, WS2, WS3, WS4, WS4_ROUTE, WS4_UI, WS7A). Five are
> deferred to scoped child specs. One (WS9) remains here as the Phase 1.1 closing gate
> but is blocked until the child specs reach implementation completion. See the
> **Amendment 2026-04-24** section below for the full dependency rewrite.

## Overview

This EXEC-SPEC scaffolds Phase 1.1 as a service-led contract migration with three bounded direct-coupling exception slices. The architectural rule is simple: internal DTO envelope work moves first; any change that already passes through a live route or UI consumer must move as a named exception slice with its paired tests, or defer to Phase 1.2.

The scaffold intentionally does not prescribe granular file edits. That belongs to `/build-pipeline` and the domain skills it dispatches.

## Amendment 2026-04-24 — Scope Deferral to Child Specs

### What triggered this amendment

A mid-execution blast-radius reality check (recorded at `.claude/skills/build-pipeline/checkpoints/PRD-070.json § blast_radius_reality_check_2026-04-24`) compared PRD-070 §258 and the workstream definitions below against the actual codebase. It surfaced four classes of structural gaps per deferred workstream (DTO type home missed, Zod wire/DTO partition undecided, paired UI component missed, new test-file birth not flagged as create-not-modify), a UI render-pattern decision under-specified (`formatCents(.value)` vs deferred `<FinancialValue>` component), a services/visit/dtos.ts serialization hazard between WS5 and WS6 not surfaced at the PRD layer, and a complexity-tier conflation (WS4's tier-1 rename vs WS5/WS6's tier-2 shape conversion) that made the slices look equal-weight when they are not. Additionally, user directive 2026-04-24 excluded `app/review/start-from-previous/page.tsx` (a `'use client'` demo/design-review surface with "Mock Data" fixtures) from the WS5_UI scope — EXEC-070 §442 had originally pulled it in under an unverified "shipped consumer" assumption.

Fixing these in-place would trigger per-workstream escalation per PRD-070 §2.3 / NFR-7. A scoped child PRD with the corrected blast radius is cleaner and more truthful than amending the parent mid-execution.

### Retained under EXEC-070 (completed, except WS9)

| Workstream | Status |
|---|---|
| WS0 — Planning Lock | ✅ Shipped |
| WS1 — Shared Contract Foundation | ✅ Shipped — `lib/financial/{schema,rounding}.ts` + tests |
| WS2 — Internal Service DTO Envelope Bundle | ✅ Shipped — player-financial, loyalty, mtl, table-context |
| WS3 — Rating-Slip Core | ✅ Shipped |
| WS4 — Rating-Slip Modal Rename Service Core | ✅ Shipped — includes SQL migration `20260424024019_prd070_rename_modal_bff_total_cash_out.sql` |
| WS4_ROUTE — Modal Route Slice | ✅ Shipped (landed atomically with WS4) |
| WS4_UI — Modal UI Slice | ✅ Shipped (landed atomically with WS4) |
| WS7A — Shift-Intelligence Phase Split Decision Record | ✅ Shipped — JSDoc carve-outs on `services/shift-intelligence/{dtos,schemas}.ts` |
| WS9 — Cross-Service Verification and Phase Exit Package | ⏸ Blocked on child specs |

### Deferred to child specs

|---|---|---|
| WS5 — Visit Recent-Sessions Cents Service Core | FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 → PRD-072 | ✅ Phase 1.1b complete (labeling only; /100 preserved) |
| WS5_ROUTE — Visit Recent-Sessions Route Boundary Slice | FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 → PRD-072 | ✅ Phase 1.1b complete (1 smoke test; full matrix Phase 1.2) |
| WS5_UI — Visit Recent-Sessions UI Consumer Slice (narrowed — `/review/` excluded) | FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 → PRD-072 | ✅ Phase 1.1b complete (formatDollars preserved; formatCents Phase 1.2) |
| WS6 — Visit Live-View Cents Canonicalization Slice | FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 → PRD-072 | ✅ Phase 1.1b complete (labeling only; /100 preserved) |
| WS7B — Shift-Intelligence Authority Routing Implementation | FIB-FIN-SHIFT-001 → PRD-073 | ✅ Complete — PRD-073 shipped 2026-04-25 (commit 11e63758) |

### Intake artifacts

- FIB-H (prose): `docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/intake/FIB-H-FIN-CENTS-001-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.md`
- FIB-S (structured JSON): `docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/intake/FIB-S-FIN-CENTS-001-visit-anchored-cents-envelope.json`
- WS7B intake + PRD: `FIB-H-FIN-SHIFT-001` / `FIB-S-FIN-SHIFT-001` under `docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/intake/FIB-FIN-SHIFT-001/`. PRD-073 drafted 2026-04-24 under `docs/10-prd/`. Different bounded context and different pattern (mapper helper + alert path parity, not shape conversion).

### Phase 1.1 Close Dependency Chain

Phase 1.1 does not close until all three of the following are true:

1. ~~FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 → PRD (TBD) → EXEC-SPEC (TBD) → implementation completion for WS5, WS5_ROUTE, WS5_UI (narrowed), WS6~~ **✅ Complete — PRD-072 Phase 1.1b (commit 38d25cc1). Labeling-only scope; /100 preserved; dollar float.**
2. ~~FIB-FIN-SHIFT-001 → PRD → EXEC-SPEC → implementation completion for WS7B~~ **✅ Complete — PRD-073 shipped 2026-04-25 (commit 11e63758)**
3. ~~WS9 runs its amended verification matrix and produces the Phase 1.2 handoff package~~ **✅ Complete — WS9 closed 2026-04-25. See ws9-completion amendment.**

WS9's `depends_on` list has been rewritten to point at `child-spec:FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 (complete)` and `child-spec:PRD-073 (EXEC-SPEC pending)` in place of the deferred workstream IDs.

## Amendment 2026-04-24 (2) — Child Spec Completion: PRD-072 Phase 1.1b

**PRD-072** (`commit 38d25cc1`) shipped under the FIB-FIN-CENTS-001 authority chain and
closes the dormant WS5 / WS5_ROUTE / WS5_UI / WS6 workstreams at **Phase 1.1b labeling scope**.

### What PRD-072 delivered

| Dormant WS | Delivered | Not delivered (Phase 1.2) |
|---|---|---|
| WS5 | RecentSessionDTO fields → FinancialValue (dollar float); centsToDollars inline wrap | Remove /100; integer cents; Zod partition |
| WS5_ROUTE | Route passthrough verified clean; 1 smoke test born | Full unauthorized/404/validation matrix |
| WS5_UI | start-from-previous.tsx + modal updated with `.value` access; formatDollars preserved | formatCents migration; components/player-sessions/\_\_tests\_\_/ birth |
| WS6 | VisitLiveViewDTO fields → FinancialValue (dollar float); mappers.ts inline wrap + null guard | Remove /100; integer cents; expanded route tests |

**Phase boundary enforced:** `/100` is preserved throughout. `FinancialValue.value` is a dollar
float (e.g. 500.00, not 50000). `financialValueSchema z.number().int()` not applied.
This is correct and intentional. Phase 1.2 owns canonicalization.

2. ~~FIB-FIN-SHIFT-001 → PRD → EXEC-SPEC → implementation close for WS7B~~ **✅ Complete via PRD-073 (2026-04-25)**

## Scope

**In scope**
- Service DTO envelope migration across the 8 PRD-070 bounded contexts
- Shared completeness and schema-validation baseline
- Approved breaking rename for `totalCashOut`
- Verified visit-facing cents canonicalization slices
- Shift-intelligence authority routing and explicit keep-vs-defer decision
- Phase 1.1 blast-radius, deferral, test, and rollback planning artifacts

**Out of scope**
- Schema migrations or new tables
- Broad API/OpenAPI migration beyond named exception slices
- Reusable UI rollout beyond named direct-coupling consumers
- Phase 1.2 cleanup work except for recording its required handoff package

## Architecture Context

- Parent PRD: `docs/10-prd/PRD-070-financial-telemetry-wave1-phase1.1-service-dto-envelope-v0.md`
- Parent roadmap: `docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/actions/ROLLOUT-ROADMAP.md`
- Mandatory sign-off source: `docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/actions/WAVE-1-PHASE-1.0-SIGNOFF.md`
- Normative mapping source: `docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/actions/WAVE-1-CLASSIFICATION-RULES.md`
- Inventory source: `docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/actions/WAVE-1-SURFACE-INVENTORY.md`

## Planning Lock Resolution

### GOV-010 Prerequisite Check

- `adr_refs` in the PRD resolve to live files.
- `scaffold_ref` is not present in PRD frontmatter. Waiver approved for Phase 1.1 because the PRD explicitly names `ROLLOUT-ROADMAP.md` and `WAVE-1-PHASE-1.0-SIGNOFF.md` as the scaffold-equivalent decomposition for this rollout.

### Gate Decisions

- `GATE-070.6` resolved: Phase 1.1 keeps only the internal shift-intelligence authority-routing package in scope. Public DTO field-shape changes for anomaly and persistent alert payloads defer to Phase 1.2 because those fields already cross live HTTP/UI boundaries and the current metric set mixes currency metrics with the `hold_percent` ratio.
- `cash_obs_total` source verified: both `rpc_compute_rolling_baseline` and `rpc_get_anomaly_alerts` read `cash_out_observed_estimate_total`, so the safe Phase 1.1 authority mapping is `estimated` with source `pit_cash_observation.extrapolated`.
- `GATE-070.7` resolved: no compatibility alias is approved for `totalCashOut`. Grep across `services/`, `app/`, and `components/` found only the modal-data flow and its paired tests; no non-modal downstream consumer requires a temporary alias.
- Shift-intelligence outbound-schema posture resolved: Phase 1.1 waives new public outbound envelope schemas while public shift-intelligence field-shape changes remain deferred. `services/shift-intelligence/schemas.ts` remains request-only in this phase; if leadership reopens this scope, outbound schema work must return in an amended EXEC-SPEC with explicit service, route, and UI ownership rather than incidental mapper churn.

### Verified Direct-Coupling Inventory

| Slice | Producer files | Verified direct consumers | Existing coverage to update in-slice | Planning decision |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| WS4 `totalCashOut` rename | `services/rating-slip-modal/dtos.ts`, `services/rating-slip-modal/schemas.ts`, `services/rating-slip-modal/rpc.ts` | `app/api/v1/rating-slips/[id]/modal-data/route.ts`, `components/modals/rating-slip/rating-slip-modal.tsx` | `app/api/v1/rating-slips/[id]/modal-data/__tests__/route.test.ts`, `services/rating-slip-modal/__tests__/rpc.test.ts`, `services/rating-slip-modal/__tests__/rpc-contract.test.ts`, `services/rating-slip-modal/__tests__/rpc-security.test.ts`, `services/rating-slip-modal/__tests__/bff-aggregation.test.ts` | Hard cut rename, no alias |
| WS5 recent-sessions cents envelope | `services/visit/dtos.ts`, `services/visit/schemas.ts`, `services/visit/crud.ts` | `app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/recent-sessions/route.ts`, `components/player-sessions/start-from-previous.tsx`, `components/player-sessions/start-from-previous-modal.tsx`, `app/review/start-from-previous/page.tsx` | `services/visit/__tests__/visit-continuation.test.ts`, `services/visit/__tests__/visit-continuation.integration.test.ts`, plus new recent-sessions route-boundary and direct consumer coverage | Route/UI slice stays in Phase 1.1; review/demo surface is treated as a shipped consumer until proven otherwise |
| WS6 live-view cents envelope | `services/rating-slip/mappers.ts`, `services/rating-slip/crud.ts`, `services/visit/dtos.ts` | `app/api/v1/visits/[visitId]/live-view/route.ts` | Existing mapper/service coverage in `services/rating-slip/**`; route-boundary coverage must be added in-slice | Route/test slice stays in Phase 1.1 |
| Deferred shift-intelligence public field changes | `services/shift-intelligence/dtos.ts`, `services/shift-intelligence/mappers.ts`, `services/shift-intelligence/alerts.ts` | `app/api/v1/shift-intelligence/anomaly-alerts/route.ts`, `app/api/v1/shift-intelligence/alerts/route.ts`, `components/shift-intelligence/anomaly-alert-card.tsx`, `components/shift-dashboard/alerts-panel.tsx`, `components/admin-alerts/alerts-page-client.tsx`, `components/admin-alerts/acknowledge-alert-dialog.tsx` | `services/shift-intelligence/__tests__/anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts` exists; alerts-route boundary and consumer coverage would need new tests | Defer public shape change to Phase 1.2 and require an amended EXEC-SPEC if Phase 1.1 scope is reopened |

### Phase 1.2 Deferral Register

| Deferred field(s) | Owning bounded context | Blocking consumers | Deferral reason | Phase 1.2 obligation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `AnomalyAlertDTO.observedValue`, `baselineMedian`, `baselineMad`, `thresholdValue` | `services/shift-intelligence/` | `app/api/v1/shift-intelligence/anomaly-alerts/route.ts`, `components/shift-intelligence/anomaly-alert-card.tsx` | Public contract currently ships bare numbers; converting to envelope/union would widen a live wire/UI contract during a service-led phase | Bundle route, UI, and contract-test updates in the Phase 1.2 API envelope slice |
| `ShiftAlertDTO.observedValue`, `baselineMedian`, `baselineMad` | `services/shift-intelligence/` | `app/api/v1/shift-intelligence/alerts/route.ts`, `components/shift-dashboard/alerts-panel.tsx`, `components/admin-alerts/alerts-page-client.tsx`, `components/admin-alerts/acknowledge-alert-dialog.tsx` | Same direct-coupling issue; persistent alert consumers would need coordinated UI and HTTP changes | Land with the Phase 1.2 shift-intelligence API cleanup slice |

Notes:
- `BaselineDTO.medianValue`, `madValue`, `minValue`, and `maxValue` do not currently cross a public HTTP boundary. They remain out of the Phase 1.1 exception set.
- WS7 still owns the exhaustive `MetricType` authority-routing helper, mapper tests for every metric value, and the documented `cash_obs_total` source assertion above.

### Direct-Coupling Minimum Test Matrix

| Slice | Mandatory Phase 1.1 coverage |
| --- | --- |
| WS4 rename | Update modal-data route boundary coverage, every `services/rating-slip-modal` RPC contract/security suite, and the directly coupled modal consumer coverage that reads the renamed field |
| WS5 recent sessions | Keep unit + integration coverage for visit continuation, add route-boundary coverage for `GET /api/v1/players/[playerId]/recent-sessions`, and add/extend consumer coverage for `components/player-sessions/start-from-previous.tsx`, `components/player-sessions/start-from-previous-modal.tsx`, and `app/review/start-from-previous/page.tsx` |
| WS6 live view | Extend rating-slip mapper/service tests and add route-boundary coverage for `GET /api/v1/visits/[visitId]/live-view` |
| WS7 internal shift routing | Add authority-routing tests covering every `MetricType`, including explicit assertions that `hold_percent` remains bare-number-only, `cash_obs_total` maps to the extrapolated estimated source, and `services/shift-intelligence/__tests__/anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts` is corrected to the current `baselineGamingDay` / `baselineCoverage` response shape |
| Deferred shift-intelligence public fields | No Phase 1.1 public contract tests. Phase 1.2 owns the route/UI/component test bundle if the public field shape changes are resumed |

### Rollback Matrix Skeleton

| Change set | Files that must revert together | Revert owner | Rollback notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| WS2 internal DTO envelope bundle | Service DTOs, schemas, mappers, and service tests in `services/player-financial/`, `services/loyalty/`, `services/mtl/`, `services/table-context/` | Owning service engineer | Git revert only; no schema or data rollback |
| WS3 rating-slip core bundle | `services/rating-slip/` DTO/schema/mapper/test files not tied to live-view route scope | Rating-slip owner | Revert separately from WS6 if live-view slice must stay isolated |
| WS4 modal rename slice | `services/rating-slip-modal/**`, `app/api/v1/rating-slips/[id]/modal-data/**`, `components/modals/rating-slip/rating-slip-modal.tsx` | Rating-slip modal owner | Revert as one vertical slice; no alias fallback path exists |
| WS5 recent-sessions slice | `services/visit/**`, `app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/recent-sessions/route.ts`, directly coupled player-session consumer tests | Visit owner | Revert service + route + UI together to avoid unit mismatch |
| WS6 live-view slice | `services/rating-slip/**` live-view mapper/service files, `app/api/v1/visits/[visitId]/live-view/route.ts`, paired tests | Rating-slip owner | Revert as one route-boundary slice |
| WS7 shift-intelligence routing | `services/shift-intelligence/` mapper/helper/test files only | Shift-intelligence owner | Safe to revert independently because public DTO shape remains deferred |

## Expert Consultation Synthesis

Stage 2 expert consultation was re-run in parallel across four scopes: shared/internal services, rating-slip and visit exception slices, shift-intelligence routing/split, and WS9 verification/handoff. The assembled execution refinements below are the governing implementation detail for `/build-pipeline`.

### WS1 — Shared Contract Foundation

- Canonical types stay in `types/financial.ts`; services must not redefine `FinancialValue`, authority, or completeness unions.
- Add one shared Zod primitive helper at `lib/financial/schema.ts` and import it into service-local `schemas.ts` files instead of duplicating the invariant envelope shape.
- Baseline rule set:
  - `FinancialValue.value` is canonical cents at every Phase 1.1 service boundary.
  - `completeness.status` is always explicit; default fallback is `'unknown'`, never omission.
  - Authority and source are mapper-owned, not consumer-inferred.
  - Dollar-to-cents rounding is pinned once and reused anywhere Phase 1.1 still crosses dollar storage.
- Shared validation target:
  - `lib/financial/__tests__/schema.test.ts`

### WS2 — Internal Service DTO Envelope Bundle

- Primary targets:
  - `services/player-financial/{dtos,schemas,mappers,http}.ts`
  - `services/loyalty/{dtos,schemas,mappers,http}.ts`
  - `services/mtl/{dtos,schemas,mappers,http,view-model}.ts`
  - `services/table-context/{dtos,schemas,mappers,http}.ts`
- Table-context is not a single-root migration. It must include the already-separated financial subdomains under:
  - `services/table-context/rundown-report/`
  - `services/table-context/shift-checkpoint/`
  - `services/table-context/shift-metrics/`
- Required test posture:
  - update all touched `mappers.test.ts` files for exact `type`, `source`, and `completeness.status`
  - update touched `http-contract.test.ts` files where service DTOs cross service HTTP wrappers
  - pin dollars-to-cents behavior in the table-context cash-observation path if it still rounds at the mapper boundary
- Carve-out guidance:
  - `player-financial`: treat as clean Pattern A output conversion
  - `loyalty`: keep points, ratios, and policy knobs as documented bare-number carve-outs
  - `mtl`: keep thresholds/config values and non-currency ratios bare
  - `table-context`: keep operational bounds and ratios bare, but migrate currency estimates and observations consistently across submodules

### WS3 / WS4 / WS5 / WS6 — Exception Slice Refinements

- WS3 stays inside the rating-slip service boundary:
  - `services/rating-slip/{dtos,schemas,mappers,crud}.ts`
  - keep its service tests in `services/rating-slip/__tests__/`
  - do not absorb the live-view route slice into WS3
- WS4 exact slice targets:
  - `WS4` service core: `services/rating-slip-modal/{dtos,schemas,rpc}.ts`
  - `WS4_ROUTE`: `app/api/v1/rating-slips/[id]/modal-data/route.ts`
  - `WS4_UI`: `components/modals/rating-slip/rating-slip-modal.tsx`
  - paired tests split with ownership: service suites stay in `services/rating-slip-modal/__tests__/`; route-boundary coverage stays under `app/api/v1/rating-slips/[id]/modal-data/__tests__/`; direct UI coverage stays with the modal component
- WS5 exact slice targets:
  - `WS5` service core: `services/visit/{dtos,schemas,crud}.ts`
  - `WS5_ROUTE`: `app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/recent-sessions/route.ts`
  - `WS5_UI`: `components/player-sessions/start-from-previous.tsx`, `components/player-sessions/start-from-previous-modal.tsx`, and `app/review/start-from-previous/page.tsx`
  - keep `services/visit/__tests__/visit-continuation.test.ts` and `visit-continuation.integration.test.ts`
  - add route-boundary coverage under `app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/recent-sessions/__tests__/`
  - add review-surface coverage under `app/review/start-from-previous/__tests__/`
- WS6 exact slice targets:
  - `services/visit/dtos.ts`
  - `services/rating-slip/{mappers,crud}.ts`
  - `app/api/v1/visits/[visitId]/live-view/route.ts`
  - keep this as a route/test exception only unless a real production UI consumer is verified during implementation
  - add route-boundary coverage under `app/api/v1/visits/[visitId]/live-view/__tests__/`
- Sequencing constraint:
  - `WS3 -> WS6` is mandatory
  - `WS5` and `WS6` must not execute in parallel in code because both touch `services/visit/dtos.ts`
  - `WS4` may run in parallel with `WS5` after `WS1`
- Hidden blast-radius notes:
  - WS4 is dual-path: both `services/rating-slip-modal/rpc.ts` and the route fallback assembler materialize the financial section
  - WS5 is not route-only; both player-session UI consumers and the shipped review page duplicate the contract locally and must move in the same slice
  - `app/review/start-from-previous/page.tsx` contains hard-coded dollar-era fixtures and therefore stays inside the authoritative Phase 1.1 consumer inventory

### WS7 — Shift-Intelligence Expert Outcome

- WS7A planning targets:
  - decision record in this EXEC-SPEC
  - waiver text recorded against `services/shift-intelligence/schemas.ts` remaining request-only in Phase 1.1
  - `services/shift-intelligence/dtos.ts` doc-only carve-out / deferral JSDoc
- WS7B implementation file targets:
  - `services/shift-intelligence/mappers.ts`
  - `services/shift-intelligence/alerts.ts`
  - `services/shift-intelligence/__tests__/{mappers,alerts-mappers,anomaly-evaluation}.test.ts`
- WS7 routing rules are frozen as:
  - `drop_total -> estimated / table_session.drop`
  - `win_loss_cents -> estimated / table_session.inventory_win`
  - `cash_obs_total -> estimated / pit_cash_observation.extrapolated`
  - `hold_percent -> bare ratio, never wrapped in Phase 1.1`
- `cash_obs_total` remains static-threshold/non-anomaly-evaluated and must preserve that behavior in tests.
- Persistent alerts currently bypass the shared mapper path in `services/shift-intelligence/alerts.ts`; WS7 is incomplete unless that path shares the same routing helper or `mapShiftAlertRow`.
- Deferred public shift-intelligence posture:
  - default action is **defer to Phase 1.2**
  - only reopen if leadership explicitly reopens a Phase 1.1 public shift-intelligence exception slice and amends this EXEC-SPEC before execution
  - deferred fields: `AnomalyAlertDTO.observedValue`, `baselineMedian`, `baselineMad`, `thresholdValue`, and `ShiftAlertDTO.observedValue`, `baselineMedian`, `baselineMad`
- Mandatory remediation found during consultation:
  - `services/shift-intelligence/__tests__/anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts` is stale relative to the current anomaly service response and must be corrected in WS7B / WS9 to assert `baselineGamingDay` and `baselineCoverage` instead of the legacy `gamingDay` / `computedAt` shape before Phase 1.1 can close

### WS9 — Verification and Handoff Refinements

- Mandatory Phase 1.1 boundary/component coverage:
  - WS4 / WS4_ROUTE / WS4_UI: modal-data route test, all `services/rating-slip-modal` RPC contract/security suites, and direct consumer coverage for `components/modals/rating-slip/rating-slip-modal.tsx`
  - WS5 / WS5_ROUTE / WS5_UI: `services/visit` continuation tests, new recent-sessions route-boundary coverage, and direct consumer coverage for `components/player-sessions/start-from-previous.tsx`, `components/player-sessions/start-from-previous-modal.tsx`, and `app/review/start-from-previous/page.tsx`
  - WS6: rating-slip mapper/service coverage plus new live-view route-boundary coverage
  - WS7B: mapper/helper parity tests, retained anomaly-evaluation behavior coverage, and the corrected anomaly route-boundary suite
- Verification gates for phase close:
  - the exact verification matrix commands listed below pass:
    - `npm run test:slice:rating-slip-modal -- --runInBand`
    - `npm run test:slice:visit -- --runInBand`
    - `npm run test:slice:rating-slip -- --runInBand`
    - `npm run test:slice:shift-intelligence -- --runInBand`
    - `npx jest --runInBand --runTestsByPath app/api/v1/rating-slips/[id]/modal-data/__tests__/route.test.ts`
    - `npx jest --runInBand --runTestsByPath app/api/v1/players/[playerId]/recent-sessions/__tests__/route.test.ts`
    - `npx jest --runInBand --runTestsByPath app/api/v1/visits/[visitId]/live-view/__tests__/route.test.ts`
    - `npx jest --runInBand --runTestsByPath components/modals/rating-slip/__tests__/rating-slip-modal.test.tsx`
    - `npx jest --runInBand --runTestsByPath components/player-sessions/__tests__/start-from-previous.test.tsx`
    - `npx jest --runInBand --runTestsByPath components/player-sessions/__tests__/start-from-previous-modal.test.tsx`
    - `npx jest --runInBand --runTestsByPath app/review/start-from-previous/__tests__/page.test.tsx`
    - `npx jest --runInBand --runTestsByPath services/shift-intelligence/__tests__/anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts`
  - `npm run type-check`
  - `npm run lint`
  - `npm run build`
  - grep gate: zero live-code `totalChipsOut` under `services/`, `app/`, and `components/`
- Handoff package must include:
  - completed rollback matrix for WS2–WS7
  - exact commands and suites run for Phase 1.1 verification
  - explicit Phase 1.2 deferred shift-intelligence route/UI/component inventory
  - the pinned dollar-to-cents rounding test file and case names for Wave 2 replication

## Workstream Skeleton

### WS0 — Planning Lock

Bounded context: cross-context governance

Purpose:
- Resolve the Phase 1.1 gates before code starts
- Freeze what is in Phase 1.1 versus what is deferred
- Convert the PRD blast-radius/test requirements into execution-owned tables

Required artifacts:
- Verified pass-through consumer tables
- Phase 1.2 deferral register
- Route/UI minimum test matrix
- Rollback matrix with ownership

### WS1 — Shared Contract Foundation

Bounded context: cross-context financial contract

Purpose:
- Establish the one Phase 1.1 baseline for envelope schema handling, completeness handling, and rounding replication
- Prevent each service from inventing its own envelope mechanics

### WS2 — Internal Service DTO Envelope Bundle

Bounded contexts:
- `services/player-financial/`
- `services/loyalty/`
- `services/mtl/`
- `services/table-context/`

Purpose:
- Execute the service-led part of the phase where no verified direct-coupling exception is required

### WS3 — Rating-Slip Core DTO Envelope Bundle

Bounded context:
- `services/rating-slip/`

Purpose:
- Migrate the core rating-slip envelope logic that is not itself the direct-coupling exception
- Prepare the bounded context for the linked live-view slice

### WS4 / WS4_ROUTE / WS4_UI — Rating-Slip Modal Rename Slice

Bounded contexts:
- `services/rating-slip-modal/` (`WS4`)
- Directly coupled modal-data route (`WS4_ROUTE`)
- Directly coupled modal UI consumer only (`WS4_UI`)

Purpose:
- Land the approved `totalCashOut` rename as a bounded service/API/UI slice with explicit executor ownership

Constraint:
- No unrelated modal-data reshaping
- No broad Phase 1.2 route work hidden inside this slice

### WS5 / WS5_ROUTE / WS5_UI — Visit Recent-Sessions Canonicalization Slice

Bounded contexts:
- `services/visit/` (`WS5`)
- Verified recent-sessions route (`WS5_ROUTE`)
- Verified recent-sessions UI consumers only (`WS5_UI`)

Purpose:
- Remove the visit-side dollar pre-conversion and ship cents envelopes where the contract already passes through unchanged
- Keep `app/review/start-from-previous/page.tsx` inside the authoritative Phase 1.1 consumer set until code proves it is not a shipped surface

### WS6 — Visit Live-View Canonicalization Slice

Bounded contexts:
- `services/rating-slip/`
- Verified live-view route/test consumers only

Purpose:
- Remove the remaining rating-slip-owned visit pre-conversion path and ship the live-view cents envelope slice

### WS7A / WS7B — Shift-Intelligence Split and Authority Routing

Bounded context:
- `services/shift-intelligence/`

Purpose:
- `WS7A`: resolve the PRD conditional branch before any public-field work starts
- `WS7B`: land the internal routing implementation that remains after the public-field deferral

Constraint:
- If public-field shape changes defer, `WS7A` still records the waiver and `WS7B` still lands the routing artifacts that remain in Phase 1.1

### WS9 — Cross-Service Verification and Handoff

Bounded context: release verification

Purpose:
- Validate the phase exit against PRD-070, not just repo compilation
- Produce the Phase 1.2 handoff package from the actual deferrals and exceptions

## Dependency Rules

- No implementation work starts before WS0 closes.
- No service bundle starts before WS1 establishes the shared contract baseline.
- WS4 / WS4_ROUTE / WS4_UI, WS5 / WS5_ROUTE / WS5_UI, and WS6 are bounded exception slices and cannot widen their consumer set without re-approval.
- Deferred public shift-intelligence work is not part of the active execution graph in this revision. Reopening it requires a lead-architect amendment plus checkpoint update before execution.
- WS9 is the only workstream allowed to close the phase.

## Phase Exit Requirements

WS9 must prove all of the following before Phase 1.1 can close:
- Every in-scope currency DTO field is either wrapped, carved out, or explicitly deferred
- Every approved direct-coupling exception has paired route/UI/component coverage
- The `totalCashOut` rename and the visit-facing cents transitions are documented in the rollback matrix
- The Phase 1.2 handoff package names every deferred field, owner, and paired consumer obligation

## Build-Pipeline Handoff

`/build-pipeline` should treat this scaffold as the workstream boundary source, then let domain skills expand each workstream with implementation detail:
- `backend-service-builder` for `WS2`, `WS3`, `WS4`, `WS5`, `WS6`, `WS7B`, and any reopened deferred public shift-intelligence service DTO / mapper / schema work
- `api-builder` for dedicated route-boundary sub-workstreams `WS4_ROUTE` and `WS5_ROUTE`
- `frontend-design-pt-2` for dedicated UI-consumer sub-workstreams `WS4_UI` and `WS5_UI`
- `lead-architect` for `WS7A` planning-only split decisions and deferral/waiver records
- `qa-specialist` for slice verification and the phase-exit package
- If deferred public shift-intelligence work is ever reopened, `/build-pipeline` must amend the EXEC-SPEC first and create ownership-aligned service, route, and UI workstreams before execution resumes.
